scores.publication: Tanigawa Y et al. PLoS Genet (2022)
scores.license: PGS Catalog terms of use; cite the source publication

Walking duration reflects opportunity, occupation, environment, disability, health, preferences, and device or questionnaire methods as well as genetics.
This score must not be used to infer motivation, discipline, or exercise capacity.
Measured steps, walking time, pace, and functional testing are more actionable.

scores.publication: Prive F et al. Am J Hum Genet (2022)
scores.license: PGS Catalog terms of use; cite the source publication
limitations: No compatible individual reference distribution is bundled, so no percentile or above/below-average claim is returned; the score is raw only.
Sleep opportunity, work, caregiving, light, stress, illness, medication, caffeine, and sleep disorders can outweigh inherited tendency.
Longer genetically predicted duration is not automatically better sleep or better recovery.
A sleep diary, wearable trends, symptoms, and clinical testing when indicated are more actionable.

scores.publication: Maukonen M et al. J Biol Rhythms (2020)
scores.license: PGS Catalog terms of use; cite the source publication
limitations: The modeled event orientation is not converted into a morning/evening label without a compatible calibrated implementation.
Chronotype is a tendency, not a fixed schedule or a sleep-disorder diagnosis.
Age, light exposure, work, caregiving, travel, and behavior materially influence observed sleep timing.
Several weeks of sleep midpoint and preferred free-day timing are more directly actionable.

scores.publication: Tanigawa Y et al. PLoS Genet (2022)
scores.license: PGS Catalog terms of use; cite the source publication
limitations: Research-only and intentionally non-directional: no percentile, higher/lower label, or prediction of intelligence is returned.
A score for one cohort-specific test does not measure general intelligence, creativity, judgment, knowledge, learning potential, or personal worth.
Education, culture, language, health, opportunity, test context, and population structure materially affect the phenotype and model.
Never use this score for education, employment, insurance, eligibility, relationship, or other high-impact decisions.

scores.publication: Tanigawa Y et al. PLoS Genet (2022)
scores.license: PGS Catalog terms of use; cite the source publication
limitations: Research-only and intentionally non-directional: no loneliness tendency, percentile, or personality label is returned.
Loneliness is a lived state shaped strongly by relationships, environment, health, culture, access, and current circumstances.
The model cannot predict relationship quality, social ability, belonging, or future mental health for an individual.
Never use this score for clinical, employment, insurance, eligibility, relationship, or other high-impact decisions.

scores.publication: Tanigawa Y et al. PLoS Genet (2022)
scores.license: PGS Catalog terms of use; cite the source publication

Never use this score for employment, insurance, eligibility, relationship, or other high-impact decisions.
